bars.description: A sophisticated bar at the heart of the lobby. Curated cocktail list, extensive wine cellar, and light bites. Perfect for business meetings or pre-dinner drinks.

treatments.name: Swedish Relaxation Massage

treatments.cancellationPolicy: Cancel at least 4 hours in advance.

treatments.cancellationPolicy: Cancel at least 4 hours in advance.

treatments.name: Executive Revive Ritual
treatments.description: Designed for the business traveller — express facial, scalp massage, and foot reflexology in a focused 90-minute session.
treatments.cancellationPolicy: Cancel at least 4 hours in advance.

treatments.name: Hammam Steam & Scrub
treatments.description: Traditional hammam experience — steam room, exfoliating body scrub, and foam massage.
treatments.cancellationPolicy: Cancel at least 2 hours in advance.

classes.name: Morning Vinyasa Flow
classes.description: Dynamic yoga flow class in our rooftop studio overlooking the city.

packages.name: Urban Escape Package
packages.description: A 3-hour retreat from city life: Swedish massage, hammam, and a healthy lunch by the rooftop pool.

options.name: Airport Arrival Transfer

options.name: Airport Departure Transfer

options.name: Colombo City Chauffeur
options.description: Chauffeur-driven car for meetings, shopping, or exploring Colombo.

experiences.name: Colombo Street Food Walking Tour
experiences.description: Explore Colombo's vibrant food scene on a guided walk through Pettah Market, kottu stalls, and heritage bakeries. Taste 8–10 local dishes along the way.

experiences.name: Colonial Fort & Pettah Heritage Walk
experiences.description: Discover Colombo's Dutch and British colonial architecture, the bustling bazaars of Pettah, and the city's hidden temples with a knowledgeable local guide.

localPlaces.name: Galle Face Green
localPlaces.description: A five-hectare oceanfront promenade stretching 500 m along the coast — Colombo's beloved public gathering space for kite flying, street food stalls, and sweeping Indian Ocean views. At dusk, vendors set up selling isso wade (prawn fritters), corn on the cob, and fresh king coconut; the atmosphere is electric.
localPlaces.hotelNote: Arrive at sunset (around 18:15) for the best light and the liveliest crowd. The Galle Face Hotel veranda just next door is perfect for a sundowner.

localPlaces.name: Gangaramaya Temple
localPlaces.description: One of Colombo's most important Buddhist temples, built on the banks of Beira Lake in 1885. The complex blends Sri Lankan, Thai, Indian, and Chinese architectural styles and houses an eclectic museum of donated offerings — from vintage cars to ivory carvings. The lake itself is home to the floating Seema Malaka meditation hall, a masterpiece by architect Geoffrey Bawa.
localPlaces.travelTime: 5 min by tuk-tuk or 18 min walk
localPlaces.hotelNote: Remove shoes before entering. The Navam Perahera festival in February is spectacular — ask our concierge for dates.

localPlaces.name: Pettah Market & Colombo Fort
localPlaces.description: Colombo's oldest and most atmospheric bazaar district, where each street specialises in a different trade — spices, electronics, textiles, jewellery, and more. Adjacent Colombo Fort retains Dutch and British colonial architecture, anchored by the restored Dutch Hospital Shopping Precinct, now a hub of boutique restaurants and bars.
localPlaces.hotelNote: Best visited on a weekday morning. Ask our concierge for a local guide — it makes a world of difference navigating the maze of streets.

localPlaces.name: National Museum of Colombo
localPlaces.description: Sri Lanka's largest museum, founded in 1877 and housed in a grand Italianate building in the Cinnamon Gardens district. Its galleries span pre-historic artefacts, the regalia of the last Kandyan king, colonial-era maps, and a superb collection of traditional masks and metalwork. A combined ticket includes the adjacent Natural History Museum.
localPlaces.hotelNote: Allow 1.5–2 hours minimum. Pair the visit with a stroll through adjacent Viharamahadevi Park — Colombo's largest public green space.

localPlaces.name: Independence Square
localPlaces.description: A striking neo-Kandyan monument and landscaped park in the leafy Cinnamon Gardens neighbourhood commemorating Sri Lanka's independence in 1948. The surrounding Arcade Independence Square complex (a restored colonial building) houses boutique fashion designers, gem stores, and artisan cafes.
localPlaces.hotelNote: The park is ideal for an early morning jog. Our chauffeur can combine a visit here with the National Museum for a full cultural half-day.

localPlaces.name: Dutch Hospital Shopping Precinct
localPlaces.description: Colombo's oldest surviving Dutch colonial building (c. 1600s), meticulously restored into a stylish dining and retail precinct within Colombo Fort. The atmospheric courtyard is lined with upscale restaurants, artisan coffee roasters, and boutique stores, making it the city's most refined evening destination.
localPlaces.hotelNote: Ministry of Crab and Nuga Gama are standout dinner picks here. Reserve in advance — both are popular with international visitors.
